fields.description:
## Why
R5's new kernel sub-answer (asserted_capability_confirmed) is only useful if the judge is reliable: an unstable judgment injects noise into the shared pass-2 vocabulary. Order-effect and self-consistency failures are the documented LLM-judge failure modes (order-swap flip 82.5%, ACL 2024).

## What
Judge-reliability evaluation: (a) 3-vote self-consistency of asserted_capability_confirmed over ~50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort), requiring Fleiss kappa >= 0.6; (b) prompt-order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion: the CANDIDATE rubric draft is authored HERE as an experiment artifact (committed under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/, not the packaged reviewers/ tree), so no circular dependency on R3 - R3 later lands the draft, corrected per these results, as the packaged rubric. Live LLM calls (billable), bounded by the small sample.

## Scope
- docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ (eval driver + runs/ output + writeup).
- Consumes E1's adjudication corpus; candidate rubric on an experiment branch; no packaged changes land from this story.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 3-vote self-consistency over >=50 capability-cohort findings is recorded with Fleiss kappa computed; kappa >= 0.6 passes, below fails and is recorded as an R5 blocker.
- [ ] Order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion is recorded (verdict agreement across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans).
- [ ] A docs/experiments/ writeup records prompts-as-run, votes, and kappa math (the input to R5's and R3's gate rows).
